sink_impls.notes: Primary human-readable output; instantiated via Standard::sink / Standard::sink_with_path
JSON-lines output; instantiated via JSON::sink / JSON::sink_with_path
Count/path-only output; instantiated via Summary::sink / Summary::sink_with_path
Closure-based sink for UTF-8 decoded lines; not used in production dispatch
Closure-based sink with lossy UTF-8 decoding; not used in production dispatch
Closure-based sink for raw bytes; not used in production dispatch
Blanket forwarding impl for mutable references
Blanket forwarding impl for boxed sinks

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search
call_chain.notes: Entry point; branches on should_decompress/should_preprocess, then delegates to search_path or search_preprocessor/search_decompress

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_path
call_chain.notes: Opens the file and calls SearchWorker::search_reader

call_chain.name: SearchWorker::search_reader
call_chain.notes: Delegates to the free-function search_reader, passing self.printer

call_chain.name: search_reader (free fn, dispatch point)
call_chain.notes: Matches on Printer<W> enum variants to obtain the concrete Sink via printer.sink_with_path(), then calls Searcher::search_reader

call_chain.name: Searcher::search_reader
call_chain.notes: Core searcher method; drives the search loop calling Sink::matched / Sink::context etc.

change_impact_map.change: Create new file. Define CSV<W>, CSVBuilder, CSVSink<M,W> structs. Implement impl Sink for CSVSink<M,W> with at minimum the matched() method. Add sink() and sink_with_path() constructors on CSV<W>.
Add 'mod csv;' module declaration and pub-use re-exports for CSV, CSVBuilder, CSVSink alongside the existing standard/json/summary re-exports (currently around lines 63-76).
Add CSV(grep::printer::CSV<W>) variant to the Printer<W> enum.
Add match arm for Printer::CSV in the free fn search_path to call csv.sink_with_path(matcher, path) and pass the resulting CSVSink to searcher.search_path().
Add match arm for Printer::CSV in the free fn search_reader to call csv.sink_with_path(matcher, path) and pass the resulting CSVSink to searcher.search_reader().
Add CLI flag (e.g. --csv) and construct Printer::CSV(grep::printer::CSV::new(wtr)) at the point where Printer variants are selected before SearchWorkerBuilder::build is called.
